span.p: One document / known small set, or a whole corpus?

span.p:
Use the retrieval-strategy tree
(long-context / Files API / RAG)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Does the task need the visual layer?
layout / tables / figures / handwriting / charts

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Native PDF / image to a vision-capable model
confirm vision support in the capability map

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Pre-extract text (OCR/parse), send the text
cheaper, you control chunking

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Same document referenced across many requests/turns?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Files API — upload once, reference by id
pairs with native OR extracted input

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Send inline this request
native bytes or extracted text
